terms.description: A four-digit Australian postcode.
See the endpoint description above.
See the endpoint description above.
See the endpoint description above.

The point's type. There are six of these enumerated values, to select from: 
 * UPL: Parcel lockers. 
 * R_SPB: A red street post box. 
 * C_SPB: Combined street post boxes (Both red and yellow boxes together). 
 * PO: An Australia Post outlet. 
 * OS: Outstation (These are a part of a post office not in the same physical building. For example, a separate building dedicated to PO boxes.) 
 * DC: A delivery centre.
A coordinate representing the angular distance in degrees, minutes and seconds, of a point east or west of the Greenwich Meridian, expressed as a double.
A coordinate representing the angular distance in degrees, minutes and seconds, of a point north or south of the equator, expressed as a double.
The radius in kilometres, to search around the provided latitude/longitude coordinate point. The default value is 50, and the hard limit is 1000Km.
A comma-delimited string of service codes by which to filter the returned list of matching locations. **Note: ** A list of current services is available through the getServices endpoint.
The maximum number of results to return. The default value is 5.
A comma-delimited list of service codes by which to filter the returned list of matching locations.
A point object is a descriptive record of an individual Australia Post outlet or facility, that includes location, contact and service availability data.
An object that contains full street address information for a specific Australia Post outlet or facility.
An object that contains full street address information for a specific Australia Post outlet or facility.
A unique numeric identification code that represents a point to which Australia Post delivers or receives deliveries.

The usage_type field specifies the access level for a location point.
For parcel lockers (type=UPL):
* "1": Accessible to both registered and unregistered users.
* "2": Accessible only to registered users.
The name of the point, described as a generic type. For example, "Post Office".
Indicates address instructions.
An Australian fax number.
An object containing the precise coordinate location of an Australia Post outlet or facility.
A hatch is a customer service window in an Australia Post parcel pickup location. If a point has an associated hatch, this field may contain extra information specific to that hatch.
An array of objects that describe the hours of operation or availability of services at a location.
A unique identification code for a specific Australia Post asset or facility (See the type field below.).
The name of the point in all uppercase characters. For example, "MELBOURNE GPO POST SHOP".

A code to identify the owner entity of the assets, facilities or services referred to by the location_code. This value is currently always "AP" (Australia Post), however, this may be expanded in future to include other entities.
An Australian telephone number.
A comma-delimited string of service IDs that identify the services available at a point. For more information about services, see the getServices endpoint

A single enumerated value denoting a service type available at a requested location. When multiple types are available at one location, a separate point object is returned for each one. There are six possible types: 
 * UPL: Parcel lockers. 
 * R_SPB: A red street post box. 
 * C_SPB: Combined street post boxes (Both red and yellow boxes together). 
 * PO:  An Australia Post outlet. 
 * OS: Outstation (These are a part of a post office not in the same physical building. For example, a separate building dedicated to PO boxes.) 
 * DC:  A delivery centre.

A unique numeric identification code that represents a parent Australia Post facility, and all of the child point objects (assets or facilities) associated with it. The facility and its point objects all share the same work_centre_id, but each point is unique.
An Address object contains full street address information for a specific Australia Post outlet or facility.
Type of address. There are three possible enumerated values: STREET_ADDRESS, PARCEL_COLLECT and COLLECTION_POINT.
Address line one.
Address line two.
Address line three.
An Australian suburb name.
An Australian state name.
A four-digit Australian postcode.
An alphabetical two-digit country identification code, as specified in the ISO 3166-2 standard.
The name of the country (as written in English), represented by the country_code field.
An object containing the precise coordinate location of an Australia Post outlet or facility.
A unique numeric identification code that represents a point to which Australia Post delivers or receives deliveries.
Indicates address instructions.

This object describes the precise location of an Australia Post outlet or facility. This object always returns latitude and longitude values for a point, although requests to the getPointsByGeoCoordinates endpoint will also include a distance value.
The global positioning latitude coordinate for a location. For example, 144.9696466.
The global positioning longitude coordinate for a location. For example, -37.8126618.

The distance in kilometres to a matching point from the coordinates provided in the endpoint request. **Note:** This field is displayed in the results for all endpoints that require you to provide longitude and latitude coordinates.
An Hour object describes a range of date and time-related information for a specific Australia Post outlet or facility.
The end date of an operating period, when non-standard hours are observed. The date is returned in the format DD/MM/YYYY. For example, "30/06/2016".
The time when an outlet or facility closes on a particular day, in the format hh:mm. For example, 23:15.
The start date of an operating period, when non-standard hours are observed. The date is returned in the format DD/MM/YYYY. For example, "29/06/2016".
The time when an outlet or facility opens on a particular day, in the format hh:mm. For example, 09:00.

The hour type, representing the hours of operation at a location. There are six possible enumerated values: 
 * HOURS: Standard operating hours when a location is open. 
 * LUNCH: The lunchtime period when a location is closed. 
 * HATCH: The hours when the parcel pickup customer service counter is open. 
 * SPECIAL_HOURS   Non-standard operating hours at a location. 
 * SPECIAL_CLOSED  Non-standard closing times at a location. 
 * SPECIAL_LUNCH: A non-standard time when a location is closed for the lunchtime period. 
 **Note: ** Times are specific to the location of the point, expressed as local time at that point.
Day of the week, enumerated from Monday (0) to Sunday (6).

A Service object describes a specific service that might be available from an Australia Post outlet or facility.  **Note:** 
 Not all outlets and facilities offer all of the services returned by the getServices endpoint.
The name of the service.
The name of the service expressed as an enumerated value.
A value indicating if a customer must first make an appointment for the requested service.
An array of objects that describe the service category to which a specific service belongs.
A value dictating whether or not a specific service will be presented in the response to a service query.

A code to identify the owner entity of the assets, facilities or services referred to by the location_code. This value is currently always "AP" (Australia Post), however, this may be expanded in future to include other entities.
A unique number identifying a specific service that is available at a location. For example, "99" that identifies the PostBillPay service.
The URL-encoded equivalent of the description field.
This object contains the name of a service category used to group services of a similar type.
The URL-encoded equivalent of the name field.
The name of the service category to which a service belongs.

An array of point objects. A point object is a data structure returned from calls to Locations API endpoints, and represents the location of a specific Australia Post outlet or facility. The number of point objects returned depends on the endpoint used, and the parameters provided in the request.

An array of service objects. A service object is a data structure returned from calls to the /services endpoint, and represents an Australia Post service that might be available at one of its outlets or facilities. There are no parameters by which to filter the service objects returned from the /service endpoint. 
 
 **Note:** Not all outlets and facilities offer all services, and the number of service objects returned may change if Australia Post increases or decreases the number of services it offers.

terms.term: Sends the delivery event to Australia Post

terms.description: Article details.
The Article Id.
The Full Barcode.
External tracking url to allow tracking via 3rd party carrier.
Image attachments showing details of a delivery (e.g. for damaged article).
A base64 encoded PNG image of the recipients signature. Restricted to 10240 bytes.
The type of image format of the attachment content.
Audit details.
Event Source Name.
Event ID.
Delivery details.
The 4-digit Australian Postcode to where the article has been sent
Details of the System where the event originated.
An event originating in a partner's system, which AP is to be informed of.
The code indicating which type of event has occurred.
Text describing the event type reason.
Text or comments describing the event reason as entered by user.
The timestamp indication when the reason occurred in ISO 8601 Offset from UTC format.
Facility details
Location details
Work Centre Code.
Location Name.
The person associated with the origin of the event.
The identifier of the sending person.
The details of the delivery.
The name of the person signing for acceptance of the delivery.
The type of ID presented.
The date of birth of the recipient (ddMMyyyy).
Elements relating to the specific Product Service Codes, e.g. Service Codes 81, 82 and 83 are for alcohol and require ID to be presented in some Australian States

The type of ID presented. Valid types are: Australian Driver License,Red or Orange MyPost card with address, Keypass or other Government-issued Proof of Age card, Australian or international passport, Digital iD.
Was ID of the receiver made available at the time of delivery?
Was ID of the receiver sighted by the person making the delivery?
Was the recipient over 18? Relevant where the Service Code on the manifest is one of 81, 82, 83.
The System where the event originated.
The identifier of the sending system.
The name of the sending system.
